title: Garlicky Crunchy Zhejiang Split Peas Stew
description: A comforting, garlicky stew with split peas and a crunchy sesame topping, inspired by Zhejiang cuisine and perfect for a dairy‑free slow cooker feast.
instructions: Rinse the split peas under cold water until the water runs clear, then set aside.
In a wok or large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and sauté the minced garlic and ginger until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery to the wok, stir‑frying for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Transfer the aromatics to a slow cooker, then add the rinsed split peas, water, vegetable broth, soy sauce, and rice wine.
Set the slow cooker to low and cook for 8–9 hours, or to high for 4–5 hours, until the peas are tender and the stew has thickened.
About 30 minutes before serving, stir the stew and taste; season with salt if needed.
In a small bowl, toast the sesame seeds over medium heat until golden, about 2 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent burning.
Serve the hot stew in bowls, sprinkling the toasted sesame seeds on top for crunch.
Optional: garnish with chopped fresh cilantro for added aroma.
Enjoy your garlicky, crunchy, dairy‑free Zhejiang split pea stew!
